meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Scale Your Development
content.seo_description: Stop building from scratch. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance projects and increase your profit margins.
content.answer_block: To scale as a freelancer, stop manual configuration. Deploy a production-ready SaaS starter kit containing authentication, database schemas, and payment integration. By pairing Antigravity’s architectural scaffolding with Claude Code’s autonomous execution, you reduce build times from weeks to hours, ensuring consistent quality and predictable delivery for every client.
content.intro:
The cold sweat of a looming deadline is a familiar companion for the solo dev. You’re trapped in a cycle of repetitive boilerplate: setting up Auth0, configuring Prisma, and wrestling with Tailwind themes. Each project feels like a new mountain to climb, leaving you with no time to scale, no energy for new clients, and a stagnant revenue stream.

But the industry's top 1% don't build from scratch—they curate. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from 'bespoke artisan' to 'scalable factory.' It’s time to stop reinventing the wheel and start deploying high-value solutions at the speed of thought using the latest AI-driven architectural tools.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Blueprint
steps.description: Establish the foundational stack of your starter kit. This ensures every project has a unified structure for easier maintenance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust SaaS starter kit with Next.js 14, Supabase for auth/DB, and Stripe integration. Include a standardized folder structure for services, hooks, and components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure based on the Antigravity blueprint in the current directory.'

steps.title: Automate Database Schema Synchronization
steps.description: Use AI to ensure your database models are always in sync with your frontend types, preventing runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema that supports multi-tenancy and user-role management, optimized for Supabase/PostgreSQL.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Apply the generated Prisma schema and execute a migration to the local development database.'

steps.title: Implement Identity and Access Management
steps.description: Don't waste time on auth logic. Automate the integration of secure session handling and protected API routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create reusable React hooks for Supabase authentication, including login, signup, and protected route wrappers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the authentication hooks and wrap the dashboard layout with a session check.'

steps.title: Integrate Production-Grade Payments
steps.description: Set up Stripe webhooks and checkout sessions to ensure your client's SaaS is monetized from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Stripe webhook handler for subscription events and a client-side checkout session creation utility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure the API routes for Stripe webhooks and test the checkout flow integration.'

steps.title: Finalize CI/CD and Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Automate the path to production so you can hand off the project to the client with a single click.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure the CI/CD pipeline and verify the deployment script against the Vercel CLI.'

pro_tips: Always maintain your starter kit in a private repo; treat it like a product, not just a folder of code.
Use 'feature-flagging' within your kit so you can toggle modules on or off based on client requirements.
Force Claude Code to write unit tests for every new feature generated; it prevents long-term technical debt.
Standardize your environment variables naming convention across all projects to ensure your Vercel/Supabase config never breaks.
Create a 'Documentation-as-Code' README generator within your kit so clients get a custom manual automatically.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is finalized, create a template repository. Clone this for every new client project to reduce your setup time from 20 hours to 20 minutes.
content.conversion_block: The era of the slow-moving freelancer is over. By automating your foundation, you reclaim your time and double your output without sacrificing your sanity. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
